content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be repurposed to solve the core flaw of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods like ICM and RND: the 'curiosity vanishing' problem that occurs when familiar environments stop generating prediction errors to drive exploration. By tagging rarely accessed episodic memory traces of under-explored environment sub-regions with intrinsic reward signals, active learning memory systems can maintain consistent exploration pressure long after prediction error signals from ICM/RND decay to near-zero.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
